projects.description: A fully custom e-commerce storefront designed and built end-to-end for Ouros Designs — from brand identity to deployed code.
projects.summary: End-to-end ownership of a Shopify storefront, including custom Liquid templating, responsive UI, and performance-first frontend architecture.

button-one.description: The primary challenge was translating a brand identity into a high-converting storefront with a distinct aesthetic — all within Shopify's templating constraints. Every design decision had to balance visual impact with platform capabilities and performance requirements.

button-two.description: I approached this as a product problem first — mapping the customer journey before touching the visual layer. Custom Liquid components gave us full control over the UI while keeping the build maintainable. The result is a storefront that feels bespoke without sacrificing performance or developer ergonomics.

vd-text.copy-one: The visual language centers on minimal, high-contrast layouts that let the product photography breathe. Typography and spacing carry most of the brand weight, reducing reliance on decorative elements.
vd-text.copy-two: Every interactive state — hover, focus, cart, checkout — was designed as part of the same system, ensuring a coherent experience from first page load to purchase completion.

projects.outcome: A deployed, production-ready storefront that reflects the brand's identity while delivering a fast, conversion-optimized shopping experience.

projects.description: A curated archive of interactive digital experiences produced for Lumen Technologies — spanning campaign systems, product tools, and infographic frameworks.
projects.summary: Three years of enterprise-scale digital production: interactive infographics, guided product tools, and AI framework visualizations — each built under real deadlines and cross-functional constraints.
projects.heroImage-alt: Lumen Technologies Cloud 2.0 Infographic on display.

button-one.description: Enterprise-scale work means operating under access restrictions, multi-stakeholder reviews, and CMS constraints — all while maintaining output quality and momentum. Each project required extracting enough context from limited information to move decisively.

button-two.description: I embedded into cross-functional teams as both designer and developer, owning the full production pipeline. Tight collaboration with creative directors and copy teams kept work aligned to strategy without losing execution quality.

vd-text.copy-one: Each piece in the Lumen archive follows Lumen's brand system while pushing the boundaries of what's achievable within enterprise CMS constraints. Strong hierarchy, motion, and structured layouts communicate technical depth without overwhelming.
vd-text.copy-two: Motion design played a central role across all pieces — reinforcing narrative flow, guiding user attention, and making abstract infrastructure concepts tangible and engaging.

projects.color-theory: Lumen's palette balances warm, high-energy tones like coral and orange with cooler hues — teal and aqua — to express both innovation and reliability. The system is designed to feel technically authoritative while remaining visually accessible.
projects.outcome: Three years of production across campaign systems, product tools, and AI frameworks — each piece positioning Lumen as a technically credible, design-forward enterprise brand.
